concept: Magic / Wonder-working / The Breath of Becoming
description: Magic: the working by which one thing becomes another and no one can say how. The seed already holds the tree, the loved thing slowly turns real, the tear becomes a flower: 'whekelu' names the change itself when its craft is hidden, wonder's own handiwork. It is not power over anything. Phi refuses command-magic along with every other command; what this word names moves the way spring moves, unforced, unexplained, and on time.
sound_symbolism: 'Whe' is the unseen breath that starts the change. The complete verb 'kelu' (become) follows it, so the form is literally a becoming preceded by what cannot be seen: hidden change flowing into presence.
grammatical_notes: Noun. 'whekelu wilemo shewo ka kelu.' (Magic makes the toy become real.) A domain names its own magic by standing before it: 'lopia tholu whekelu', the nursery's magic. A worker of it is 'whekelu miona', a magic person. Kin to 'kelu' (become), the change it works; 'moluki' (transform), the change complete; and 'whemura' (dream), the mind's own unexplained making.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Impermanence wearing its festival clothes: magic is change honored as mystery rather than mastered as mechanism, met with beginner's mind.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The style's whole claim: that organic form is enchantment, the lily and the vine already halfway to another world.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Every hearth culture keeps a word for the change no craft explains — the bread that rises, the spring that returns; naming it keeps wonder in the daily round.
